post.title: The Energy Vacuum: Cannibalizing the Global Commodity Complex!!
post.excerpt: The global market is currently witnessing a paradox that defies traditional supercycle logic. While historical trends suggest that a "rising tide lifts all boats," the current 2026 energy shock is doing the exact opposite. The Brent crude is trading at $113...
post.body:
The global market is currently witnessing a paradox that defies traditional supercycle logic. While historical trends suggest that a "rising tide lifts all boats," the current 2026 energy shock is doing the exact opposite. The Brent crude is trading at $113 per barrel ( 55% since start of war ⬆️ 27th Feb), having recently tested a multi-year high of $119. While these prices are below the "panic spikes", the sustained pressure at the $100+ level is creating a structural Liquidity Evaporation that is sucking the life out of metals and agriculture markets alike. The primary driver of this shift is the Collapse of Petrodollar Recycling. In previous oil booms, high prices led to a massive influx of dollars into the global financial system as oil-exporting nations reinvested their surplus. However, with the Strait of Hormuz blockade significantly throttling the volume of exports, the total dollar revenue entering the system has cratered despite the high price per barrel. The "Petrodollar" is no longer being recycled because the physical flow of oil is restricted. This has created a systemic dollar shortage, forcing a Liquidity Drain where capital is diverted from speculative commodities into essential energy procurement and debt servicing. Metals (Gold, Silver, Copper) are the first to feel this vacuum. Copper, usually the bellwether for global growth, is signaling a sharp recessionary pivot (5.4 $/Lbs 10%). As energy costs paralyze ⬇️ manufacturing in Europe and Southeast Asia, the demand for industrial metals has decoupled from energy prices and begun to sag. Even Gold (4760 $/Oz 10%) and Silver (74.8 $/Oz 20%), ⬇️ ⬇️ traditional "Safe Havens," are struggling to hold their ground. In a global liquidity crunch, traders sell whatever they can—even the "safe" assets—to cover margin calls on energy positions and strengthen their cash reserves in an environment of hyper volatility. We are seeing a "Sell- Everything" mentality as liquidity becomes the only true asset that matters.

The Agriculture Pincer is perhaps the most dangerous manifestation of this trend. While high energy/gas costs have pushed input costs to the moon—with Urea touching $610/MT ( 30%) —⬆️ the prices of Wheat ( 2.5%), Rice ( 1%), Maize ( 6%), Soya ( 0.1%), and Palm Oil ( 8.5%) ⬆️ ⬇️ ⬆️ ⬆️ ⬆️ are beginning to weaken after sharp upside from 27th Feb. This is a classic case of Demand-Side Destruction. As the global "Liquidity Tank" empties, the purchasing power is being decimated. We are seeing a structural softening in Soybeans while Palm Oil emerged strong due to the biofuel premium. However their is collapse in discretionary spending and the inability of trade houses to secure the credit needed to move grains. The disconnect between the energy sector and the rest of the market is widening, creating a scenario where "expensive to produce" no longer translates to "expensive to sell." https://www.linkedin.com/posts/know-your-commodity_the-energy-vacuum-cannibalizing-the- global-activity-7440287191198760960-pPQS
post.author: Know Your Commodity
